original_hypothesis.title: Suspend+Unsuspend Creates Proof Receipt for Arbitrary Message Hashes Bypassing Merkle Verification
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: suspendMessages sets proofReceipt[msgHash].receivedAt to type(uint64).max (suspend) or block.timestamp (unsuspend). In processMessage, the check `bool isMessageProven = receivedAt != 0` skips _proveSignalReceived entirely if a proof receipt exists. A compromised bridge_watchdog can: (1) call suspendMessages([crafted_hash], true), (2) call suspendMessages([crafted_hash], false) which sets receivedAt=block.timestamp, (3) wait for invocation delay, (4) process a fabricated message matching the hash without any Merkle proof verification. Unlike recallMessage which checks isSignalSent, processMessage has no such additional check.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a critical design flaw in the suspend/unsuspend mechanism. The unsuspend action creates a proof receipt without proof verification. Combined with the default NEW(0) message status for all hashes, any crafted message matching an unsuspended hash can be processed.

proof_of_signal.pattern: suspendMessages sets receivedAt=block.timestamp on unsuspend; processMessage skips proof if receivedAt != 0

preconditions: bridge_watchdog key is compromised or colluding
Attacker can compute message hash for desired fabricated message
Invocation delay period passes after unsuspend

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Unsuspend sets receivedAt=block.timestamp for any msgHash; processMessage checks only receivedAt != 0 to skip proof
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: processMessage lacks isSignalSent check (unlike recallMessage), so completely fabricated messages can be processed
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No proof validation after unsuspend; no isSignalSent check in processMessage

analysis_questions: Can the attack drain the bridge's entire ETH balance?
Can the attack trigger arbitrary vault operations (mint bridged tokens)?
Does the invocation delay provide sufficient response time?
validation_requirements: Calculate maximum extractable value per attack
Verify recallMessage is NOT vulnerable (has isSignalSent check)

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised watchdog: (1) Compute hash of fabricated message targeting vault with arbitrary token mint data. (2) Suspend+unsuspend the hash. (3) After invocation delay, call processMessage with fabricated message. Bridge executes message without proof, triggering vault to mint bridged tokens or release canonical tokens.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total drain of bridge ETH and vault token holdings
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Requires pausing bridge and contract upgrade

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for suspend+unsuspend followed by fabricated message processing

technical_investigation_focus.capital_requirements: Zero for the attack itself (uses bridge's own funds)
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable: suspend+unsuspend events for non-existent messages

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
